title: Iranian Player's Asylum Twist, Clive Palmer's Political Return, Meghan Markle Shock Move | 10 News+
description: A dramatic twist in the Iranian women’s football delegation story. One of the players who was granted an emergency humanitarian visa in Australia has now made the unexpected decision to return to Iran.  Mining billionaire Clive Palmer has confirmed he will run in the next federal election, promising his biggest campaign yet despite previously spending millions without winning a seat.  A movement pushing for Western Australia to break away from the rest of the country has released a detailed manifesto outlining how the state could become an independent nation. The plan includes sweeping changes such as eliminating income tax and creating a new national system.  Meghan Markle is preparing to visit Australia alongside Prince Harry, and fans will have a rare opportunity to meet the Duchess of Sussex in person. But the chance to attend the exclusive event comes with a hefty price tag that’s raising eyebrows.  A grieving family has revealed the heartbreaking moment they discovered their mother and brother were killed in a deadly attack linked to the Iran conflict. Their story highlights the devastating human toll of escalating violence in the region. description: Tonight on 10’s Late News - Thousands have been rocked by a 4.4 magnitude earthquake that struck southern New South Wales this afternoon, three cargo ships have been hit by unknown projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, hundreds of homes and businesses have been flooded in Queensland's Bundaberg, Matt Canavan has outlined his vision for a ‘hyper Australia’ as he takes on leadership of the Nationals party, the government is warning fuel retailers they could face huge fines if they try to profit off Australians, a 600-kilogram space probe is in the process of plummeting back to earth, one of the Iranian football players granted asylum has changed her mind about seeking safety on Australian shores, Australian designer Katie Perry has won her long-running court battle against popstar Katy Perry, 2 of the 4 banks are predicting an interest rate rise on Tuesday, Melbourne has been voted the best city in the world, former prime minister Scott Morrison has been cleared over the robodebt scandal, at least six people have died and 5 others are injured after a bus fire in Switzerland, and the NAPLAN system was put to the test after a technical glitch caused a widespread outage.
